title: DNA extraction for unsorted bulk river samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbmpo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgc76","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"1baae","text":"The objective of this protocol is to propose an unsorted bulk DNA extraction methods for samples collected in aquatic environment such as kick-net samples. These community DNA extractions can then be used to describe various aspect of these communities using for example metabarcoding approaches.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":48,"length":8},{"style":"bold","offset":57,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":162,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3mn7f","text":"","type":"align-center","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5r8dg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r1ml","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4iooa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vmra","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eqjj2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a3r36","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bktib","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"768r3","text":"The protocol starts with a sample preserved in ethanol. The different steps of the protocol are:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2kish","text":"replacing the ethanol by a TNES Buffer,","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":27,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a4s05","text":"grinding the sample,","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eel60","text":"DNA extraction with the Qiagen PowerSoil Pro kit.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5udcl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cobu9","text":"This protocol has been optimized and tested on mock samples composed of macrozobenthos organisms, algae, bryophytes, leaf litter, sand, gravels, and ethanol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"align":"right","alt":"A river in a forest","guid":"EE126A5C3B8411F0B27B0A58A9FEAC02","height":254.37031150124304,"id":583048,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/jpeg","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"IMG_20210217_090804.jpg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/t3cq633e.jpg","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/t3ni633e.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/t3nh633e.webp","width":190.8644902123924}}}}

steps: These steps are done under a biosafety cabinet

DAY 2 Spin the PowerBead Pro Tube briefly to ensure that the beads have settled at the bottom. - Add up to 250 mg, of matrice in the Power Bead Pro Tube with a clean spatula. (250 µL of TNES buffer for treatment blank). - Add 800 µL, of solution CD1 . Homogenize samples thoroughly using the Geno/Grinder: 15000 rpm, °C, 10 minutes Centrifuge the PowerBead Pro Tube at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Transfer the supernatant to a clean 2 ml Microcentrifuge Tube (provided). Add 200 µL, of Solution CD2 and vortex for 5 s. Centrifuge at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Avoiding the pellet, transfer up to 700 µL, of supernatant to a clean 2 ml Microcentrifuge Tube (provided). Add 600 µL, of Solution CD3 and vortex for 5 s. Load 650 µL, of lysate onto an MB Spin Column and centrifuge at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through and repeat the previous step to ensure that all of the lysate has passed through the MB Spin Column. Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2ml Collection Tube (provided). Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column. Add 500 µL, of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column back into the same 2 ml Collection Tube. Add 500 µL, of Solution C5 to the MB Spin Column. Centrifuge at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard the flow-through. Centrifuge at 16.000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Carefully place the MB Spin Column into a new 1.5 ml Elution Tube (provided). Add 70 µL, of Solution C6 to the center of the white filter membrane. Centrifuge at 15.000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard the MB Spin Column. The DNA is now ready for downstream applications. Note : We recommend storing the DNA frozen (–30 to –15°C or –90 to –65°C) as Solution C6 does not contain EDTA.

DAY 1 Place all the equipment under UV light for 15min before starting (beaker, elastics, Day 1 mesh, bench protection paper, TNES) Empty the ethanol from the bulk sample container into a beaker using 600 µm nylon mesh. Note : when pouring ethanol or TNES, make sure the mesh is taut and pulled tight at the elastic band to avoid leaks. Note : Check that no material (especially organisms) remains on the mesh. If any remain, return them to the bottle (e.g. by flicking the top of the mesh). Add TNES buffer ( 100 millimolar (mM), Tris-HCL , 100 millimolar (mM), EDTA , 1.5 Molarity (M), NaCl ) to get a 2:1 ratio (TNES:sample) and homogenize (12 pipettings). Note : Don't forget the treatment blank: 100 mL in an empty flask. Leave the samples in TNES Overnight at 4 °C
At the end of Day 1 : - Empty the ethanol beaker in a liquid container and set aside - Put dirty elastics and mesh aside

DAY 2 Place all the equipment under UV light for 15min before starting (beaker, elastics, mesh, tongs, Day 2 spatulas, beads, 2 bench protective papers) Recover Day 1 vials and keep on ice. Empty the TNES into a beaker using 600 µm nylon mesh. Note : when pouring TNES, make sure the mesh is taut and pulled tight at the elastic band to avoid leaks. Note : Check that no material (especially organisms) remains on the mesh. If any remain, return them to the bottle (e.g. by flicking the top of the mesh). Removing TNES before grinding Add 1 x 10 mm stainless steel beads and 100g of 5 mm stainless steel beads to the flask. Clean the bench and replace the protective paper with a new one. Grind with Geno/Grinder : - 3 cycles of : 5 * (2 min + 30 sec pause), 1500 rpm - 10 min pause between each cycle. Note : Run a cycle and check the sample's condition before starting a new one, stopping when the sample is fully ground up. Inside a kick-net sample before (A) and after (B) grinding with steel beads in the Geno/Grinder Back under the biosafety cabinet, proceed directly to weighing for extraction (see the first step of Extraction section). Note : At this stage, you can recover the beads (with tweezers if necessary) from the samples and return them to their boxes in the biosafety cabinet. This step can be carried out while the extraction tubes are being filled, or afterwards. Proceed to extraction or store at 4 °C or -20 °C until extraction.

At the end of Day 2 : - Empty buffer beaker - Put dirty elastics and mesh aside - Wash the elastics in demineralized water - Decontaminate equipment (beaker + beads + tweezers + spatulas): spray with decontaminant (5% bleach, 0.5% NaOH, 0.5% detergent) and leave for 10 minutes, then rinse with demineralized water.
creator: Sophie Darinot Thomas
